title: Power and Thermal Monitoring (Cisco UCS)
exclusions: Cisco Intersight–managed (or Intersight-primary) UCS domains where most environmental statistics are consumed through the Intersight cloud or connector path rather than the classic UCSM XML statistics poll that feeds `cisco:ucs:stats` in the Splunk add-on; UCS Director or other aggregate products that re-shape kW/thermal in a different schema; C-series operating in standalone CIMC mode only and not registered in the UCSM domain you poll; and Cisco HyperFlex HX environmental or cooling data that lives in a different object and app model than the UCSM MO classes listed in this use case.
dataSources: `index=cisco_ucs` (or your org-standard index) and sourcetype `cisco:ucs:stats` from the Splunk add-on for Cisco UCS (Splunkbase 2731). Power, cooling, and fan readings appear as statistics events for `equipmentChassisStats`, `equipmentPsuStats`, `equipmentFanStats`, `equipmentFanModuleStats`, `processorEnvStats`, `computeMbTempStats`, `computeMbPowerStats`, `equipmentNetworkElementFanStats`, and `equipmentIOCardStats` — each with `classId` and a `dn` and xml-derived attribute field names the SPL normalizes with `coalesce()`.
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` ([Splunkbase 2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731), v4.3.1 or newer, supported on Splunk 9.x, 10.x, and Splunk Cloud). The on-disk name uses an underscore; older package filenames may show a hyphen, which is packaging only, not a different product.
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-24h@h
| search (classId="equipmentChassisStats" OR classId="equipmentPsuStats" OR classId="equipmentFanStats" OR classId="equipmentFanModuleStats" OR classId="processorEnvStats" OR classId="computeMbTempStats" OR classId="computeMbPowerStats" OR classId="equipmentNetworkElementFanStats" OR classId="equipmentIOCardStats")
| eval ldn=lower(dn)
| rex field=dn max_match=0 "chassis-(?<chassis_num>\d+)"
| rex field=dn max_match=0 "switch-(?<fi_side>A|B)"
| eval metric=case(
  classId="equipmentChassisStats", "chassis_ambient",
  classId="equipmentPsuStats", "psu_output_power",
  (classId="equipmentFanStats" OR classId="equipmentFanModuleStats"), "fan_rpm",
  classId="processorEnvStats", "cpu_socket_temp",
  (classId="computeMbTempStats" AND match(ldn, "front")), "mb_temp_front",
  (classId="computeMbTempStats" AND match(ldn, "rear")), "mb_temp_rear",
  (classId="computeMbTempStats" AND match(ldn, "io")), "mb_temp_io",
  (classId="computeMbTempStats"), "mb_temp_any",
  classId="computeMbPowerStats", "rail_12v",
  classId="equipmentNetworkElementFanStats", "fi_fan_rpm",
  classId="equipmentIOCardStats", "iom_temp",
  1=1, "unmapped"
)
| eval metric_val=case(
  metric="chassis_ambient", coalesce(ambientTemp, ambient_temp, ioTemp, io_temp, frontTemp, front_temp, rearTemp, rear_temp, temp, temperature, ambient, ambientC),
  metric="psu_output_power", coalesce(outputPower, output_power, outputPowerAvg, output_power_avg, outputWatts, output_watts),
  metric="fan_rpm", coalesce(speed, fan_speed, rpm, speed_avg, fanRpm, fan_rpm, measuredSpeed, measured_speed),
  metric="cpu_socket_temp", coalesce(temperature, temp),
  metric="mb_temp_front", coalesce(temperature, temp, tmrFront, tmr_front, frontTemp, front_temp),
  metric="mb_temp_rear", coalesce(temperature, temp, tmrRear, tmr_rear, rearTemp, rear_temp),
  metric="mb_temp_io", coalesce(temperature, temp, tmrIO, tmr_io, ioHTest, iohTest, iohTemp, ioh_temp),
  metric="mb_temp_any", coalesce(temperature, temp),
  metric="rail_12v", coalesce(voltage, volt, v12, v_12, power12V, power_12v, pwrVolt12, mbPwr12v, mb_p12v),
  metric="fi_fan_rpm", coalesce(speed, fan_speed, rpm, speed_avg, fanRpm, fan_rpm, measuredSpeed, measured_speed),
  metric="iom_temp", coalesce(temperature, temp, ambientEnvTemp, ambient_env_temp, envTemp, env_temp),
  1=1, null()
)
| eval metric_val=tonumber(metric_val)
| where isnotnull(metric_val) AND isnotnull(metric) AND metric!="unmapped" AND isnotnull(classId) AND isnotnull(dn)
| sort 0 - _time
| append [search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-24h@h classId="equipmentChassisStats" | eval ldn=lower(dn) | rex field=dn max_match=0 "chassis-(?<chassis_num>\d+)" | eval classId="equipmentChassisStats" | eval metric="chassis_input_power" | eval metric_val=tonumber(coalesce(inputPower, input_power, measuredPower, measured_power, powerDraw, power_draw, powerConsumed, power_consumed)) | where isnotnull(metric_val) | sort 0 - _time]
| append [search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-24h@h classId="equipmentPsuStats" | eval ldn=lower(dn) | rex field=dn max_match=0 "chassis-(?<chassis_num>\d+)" | eval classId="equipmentPsuStats" | eval metric="psu_temp" | eval metric_val=tonumber(coalesce(temperature, temp)) | where isnotnull(metric_val) | sort 0 - _time]
| sort 0 - _time
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(metric_val) as metric_val by dn, classId, metric, chassis_num, fi_side
| eval class_id=classId
| lookup ucs_environmental_thresholds class_id, metric, OUTPUT warn_low, warn_high, crit_low, crit_high, unit, notes
| fillnull value="" warn_low, warn_high, crit_low, crit_high, unit, notes
| eval wl=if((warn_low=="" OR isnull(warn_low)), null(), tonumber(warn_low))
| eval wh=if((warn_high=="" OR isnull(warn_high)), null(), tonumber(warn_high))
| eval cl=if((crit_low=="" OR isnull(crit_low)), null(), tonumber(crit_low))
| eval ch=if((crit_high=="" OR isnull(crit_high)), null(), tonumber(crit_high))
| eval has_thr=if(isnull(wl) AND isnull(wh) AND isnull(cl) AND isnull(ch), 0, 1)
| eval status=case(
  has_thr=0, "NoBaseline",
  (isnotnull(ch) AND metric_val>ch) OR (isnotnull(cl) AND metric_val<cl), "Critical",
  (isnotnull(wh) AND metric_val>wh) OR (isnotnull(wl) AND metric_val<wl), "Warn",
  1=1, "OK"
)
| eval st_sort=case(status="Critical", 1, status="Warn", 2, status="NoBaseline", 3, 1=1, 4)
| table last_seen, st_sort, status, classId, metric, metric_val, unit, notes, dn, chassis_num, fi_side
| sort 0 st_sort, -metric_val
description: Monitors live Cisco UCS Manager statistics on `cisco:ucs:stats` for power, thermal, and fan managed-object classes, compares each reading to a per-sensor `class_id+metric` lookup, and classifies the latest-24h sample per `dn` into OK, Warn, Critical, or NoBaseline for panels and optional alerts.
value: Cooling and electrical margin usually erodes gradually: inlet drift, rising chassis input watts, and fan or 12V readings outside a band you define are the earliest structured management-plane signals, separate from a guest-OS or hypervisor view. A small CSV your team can edit keeps 18C cold-aisle and 27C warm-aisle sites from being judged by a single flat temperature rule that would lie on both. One deduplicated, `dn`-keyed 24h table gives both finance a kW story and operators time to re-home workloads or open a change before throttling, brownout risk, or fabric maintenance turns into a customer-visible incident.
implementation: Turn on the Cisco UCS statistics input so `cisco:ucs:stats` is landing with the environmental MO classes, copy `ucs_environmental_thresholds.csv` and a `transforms.conf` lookup stanza onto the search tier, save the `spl` as a 24h report, then build a table panel; schedule an alert on Warn and Critical only after the lookup is tuned to your kW and thermal policy and after you are comfortable with NoBaseline as an inventory to-do, not a page.
visualization: Sortable table with `status` colours; two single values for count of rows in Warn and in Critical; bar chart of highest `chassis_input_power` or `psu_output_power` with `chassis_num` on the x-axis; optional timechart clone for kW; grey list of NoBaseline rows for lookup maintenance.

references.title: Cisco UCS Manager XML API Reference, Release 4.2 (MO and statistics model)
Integrate data with Splunk: Cisco UCS (help.splunk.com)
Cisco UCS Manager GUI User Guide, Chassis Management (thermal and power context)
Splunk documentation: transforms.conf and CSV lookups

knownFalsePositives:
• A/B or dual-PDU hand-off in the same rack: a short AC sag during automatic transfer to the other feed can show a one-poll drop in input-side watts or a rail blip; correlate with the facility BMS or electrician ticket before you RMA a supply.
• Hot-swap or FRU reseat: EEPROM discovery can read 0°C, 0 W, or 0 rpm while Cisco’s soak timer is still running—wait at least two full UCSM statistics poll intervals, then re-check the GUI and suppress during the tagged change window.
• Fan tach pinned high during a Fabric Interconnect or IOM activate/firmware wave when no fault is open in the parallel fault stream—triage to the change calendar; treat as expected cooling margin unless `faultInst` shows a new thermal or power fault code.
• Cluster leadership change or HA state move on the management plane: a single empty stats bucket for some MO classes is common; do not call a P1 on one quiet poll—compare to a `timechart count` and UCSM high-availability syslog if you have it.
• NTP or service-processor clock out of step with UCSM after a maintenance window: the order of “clear” vs “raise” in raw event sort can look inverted even when hardware is fine; the latest-by-`dn` in this use case helps, but fix clocks for Sev1 trust.
• PSU slot marked absent in inventory while the pair still provides redundant feed: watt counters can look normal while a slot flaps; confirm with a physical check before you assume the monitoring tool is wrong about power headroom to the room.
• UCS X-series IFM or 65xx line-card cooling policy diverging from 2200 IOM/5108-era experience: a single global inlet threshold may be infeasible for a warm row—split extra rows in the lookup after 30 days of site baselines, not the first day of the pilot.
• Long-uptime 32-bit-style counter semantics if someone accidentally mapped a cumulative energy field into an “instant kW” panel: a stair-step or apparent reset is often wrap, not a real ten-megawatt drop—validate the managed-object attribute in Cisco’s reference for that firmware and field name.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS (`Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs`, Splunkbase 2731, v4.3.1 or newer) installed on a Heavy Forwarder (or any tier permitted to run modular inputs) and on Search Heads for field discovery and lookups. The statistics modular input must reach `https://<ucsm-fqdn>` on 443 with a read-only user able to read equipment statistics object classes. Budget license by time-range sampling: `cisco:ucs:stats` volume grows with the MO class set you enable, poll interval, and domain size. **Never** build panels on a fictional `cisco:ucs:environmental` sourcetype that older notes sometimes mention—that string does not exist in the canonical add-on emit list; the correct feed is always `cisco:ucs:stats` with a `classId` filter for power, thermal, and fan managed object classes. If a legacy runbook or dashboard references `cisco:ucs:environmental`, treat it as a documentation bug and re-point the search.
• A dedicated UCSM service account. Permissions must cover statistics MOs such as `equipmentPsuStats`, `equipmentChassisStats`, `equipmentFanStats`, `equipmentFanModuleStats`, `processorEnvStats`, `computeMbTempStats`, `computeMbPowerStats`, `equipmentNetworkElementFanStats`, and `equipmentIOCardStats`. The human administrator account in your browser is not a substitute; wire the same user the modular input uses.
• An approved index, typically `cisco_ucs`, and a role whose `srchIndexesAllowed` includes it. Indexers and Search Heads should share the add-on for props consistency if you ever rename or clone sourcetypes in `local/`.
• Baseline: know whether your data hall runs cold-aisle (≈18–22C) or warm/ASHRAE A3-style aisle supply so you can edit the threshold lookup (below) in the first week, not the first page.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection and install the customer threshold lookup**
(1) Install `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` on the collection tier and Search Head cluster per the Splunk and Cisco app notes for your platform version.
(2) In Splunk Web, Settings → Data inputs → Cisco UCS statistics (wording can vary by minor add-on build), add or edit a modular input pointing to your UCSM cluster FQDN or virtual IP, bind the destination index to your standard (example `cisco_ucs`), and keep the add-on’s default sourcetype for statistics, which is `cisco:ucs:stats` — this is the only sourcetype this UC targets.
(3) In the input’s class list, ensure the environmental and power statistics package is enabled, not a minimal subset. After two poll cycles, run discovery: `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-1h | stats count by classId` and confirm the rows list includes at least `equipmentChassisStats`, `equipmentPsuStats`, and `processorEnvStats`. If a class is missing, the statistics profile in the add-on is too tight—expand it per vendor field guidance, wait one poll, re-run the probe.
(4) On the Search Head (or the deployable app you attach to the SH cluster), create the lookup file. Path:
`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_sh_app>/lookups/ucs_environmental_thresholds.csv`
(Replace `<your_sh_app>` with a governed app in your `serverclass`—often `cisco_ucs_ops` or a local add-on app; a dedicated non–default-app name is often cleaner for org-owned content.)
(5) Paste the literal Threshold CSV block from this step into that file. Save with a trailing newline.
(6) On the same Search Head app, add a `transforms.conf` stanza. File:
`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_sh_app>/local/transforms.conf`

```ini
[ucs_environmental_thresholds]
filename = ucs_environmental_thresholds.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 1
default_match =
max_matches = 1
```

(7) Optional: export the lookup to indexers. File: `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_sh_app>/metadata/local.meta` — if your org uses `metadata` for lookup replication, add:

```
[lookups/ucs_environmental_thresholds.csv]
export = system
```

(If you prefer the UI, Settings → Lookups can grant the operator role read access; either path is acceptable as long as `| inputlookup ucs_environmental_thresholds` works for the test user before you build the panel.)
(8) `splunk restart` the Search Head members that load the new `transforms.conf`, or verify with `splunk btool transforms list --debug` that the local stanza wins, then in Search: `| inputlookup ucs_environmental_thresholds` and confirm rows. If the command fails, the app path or `local.meta` export is wrong.
(9) The Heavy Forwarder that runs the statistics modular input does not need a copy of the CSV; enrichment is at search time.
(10) File-driven collection (alternative to the UI in step 2): on the host that will run the modular input, open `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/default/inputs.conf` and find the stanzas the vendor actually ships for Cisco UCS statistics. Copy the matching stanza into `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/local/inputs.conf`, set `disabled = 0`, set the `url` to `https://<ucsm-fqdn>`, bind `index` to your approved name (commonly `cisco_ucs`), and leave the sourcetype the stanza already assigns to that stream so the events still land as `cisco:ucs:stats`. Restart `splunkd` on that node. If your shipped file uses a different key naming than the template in this use case, follow the add-on’s text exactly—improvised stanza names fail at runtime.

Threshold CSV (literal copy for `ucs_environmental_thresholds.csv`)

```csv
class_id,metric,warn_low,warn_high,crit_low,crit_high,unit,notes
equipmentChassisStats,chassis_ambient,,,33,40,C,"Inlet or near-ambient path; tune for 18C cold aisle vs 27C warm aisle"
equipmentChassisStats,chassis_input_power,,,9000,12000,W,"Chassis input power budget example; set to ~80% of provisioned feed at that chassis"
equipmentPsuStats,psu_output_power,,,900,1000,W,"Per-PSU watt headroom; edit for 750W vs 1200W parts and n+1 policy"
equipmentPsuStats,psu_temp,,,55,70,C,"PSU internal thermistor; not comparable to inlet or CPU socket thermals"
equipmentFanStats,fan_rpm,2000,,500,20000,RPM,"Below warn means low airflow risk; pair with operState if available in your build"
equipmentFanModuleStats,fan_rpm,2000,,500,20000,RPM,"Fan module aggregate; same low-RPM logic as per-fan stats"
processorEnvStats,cpu_socket_temp,,,80,90,C,"Processor socket path; many generations approach throttle near 90C class"
computeMbTempStats,mb_temp_front,,,40,50,C,"Front motherboard sensor; usually follows supply air more than rear exhaust"
computeMbTempStats,mb_temp_rear,,,55,65,C,"Rear exhaust path; expect higher than front in normal operation"
computeMbTempStats,mb_temp_io,,,45,55,C,"IOH or IO hotspot path; NIC-heavy traffic can lift this first"
computeMbPowerStats,rail_12v,10.5,12.2,9.0,14.0,V,"12V motherboard rail; undervolt and overvolt both matter"
equipmentNetworkElementFanStats,fi_fan_rpm,2000,,500,12000,RPM,"Fabric interconnect cooling; noisy during FI PSU or line-card work"
equipmentIOCardStats,iom_temp,,,70,80,C,"IOM or FEX environmental path; short spikes during module reseat"
```

(12 baseline rows. Empty cells are unbounded in that direction: thermals and watts use high-side warn/crit; fans and the 12V row use low-side warn/crit for undervolt or under-speed. Edit every number for your facility design points.)

**Step 2 — Create the search, the dashboard panel, and an optional alert**
(1) In Search, confirm the attributes your site actually emits: `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-15m | head 20 | fieldsummary temperature outputPower inputPower speed classId` — the names you see are what you add to the `coalesce()` lists in the saved search if a field spelling differs.
(2) Paste the primary SPL from the `spl` field of this use case, save it as a report, default time Last 24 hours.
(3) Add a Table panel: show `status`, `classId`, `metric`, `metric_val`, `unit`, `chassis_num`, `dn` — the `dn` is the hand-off string for the UCSM tree walk in Step 3.
(4) The two `append` blocks in the search exist because a single `equipmentChassisStats` event can carry both thermal and watts attributes as different XML fields; without `append` you would drop half the story or need multivalue expansion. The `psu_temp` `append` does the same when temperature and output power are both present on `equipmentPsuStats`.
(5) The closing `| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(metric_val) as metric_val by dn, classId, metric, chassis_num, fi_side` is the per-sensor stability pass: a bouncing tach in one poll will not re-page if the deduplicated `metric_val` is steady across the window you schedule.

Search walkthrough
• classId, not a synthetic object label — every row in `cisco:ucs:stats` carries a Cisco managed-object `classId`. The SPL never invents a parallel field; identifiers for placement live in the dn (for example `sys/chassis-2/...`, `sys/switch-A/.../fan-1`). A flat “chassis id” is not a first-class field in the stream—`rex` recovers a chassis number when the path includes `chassis-n`.
• `coalesce()` — XML uses camelCase; some deployments or props normalizations may expose `snake_case`. The `coalesce()` list tries both so the first good number wins without you editing the TA.
• `tonumber` + `where` — drops stray non-numeric debris after an extraction change so thresholds stay numeric.
• `lookup` + four bounds — your CSV can express “too hot,” “too high watt,” “too slow fan,” or “12V out of band.” NoBaseline means the pair `(classId, metric)` is not in the file yet—show it on a grey list for CMDB work, not as a false red alarm.
• `status` order — NoBaseline is evaluated before severity so missing rows are visible without being called OK.

Companion probe (not a replacement for the full search)
`index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-1h | stats count by classId | sort -count` — use when a stakeholder claims “stats stopped” to prove whether the index is empty or the role is blind.

**Step 3 — Validate against Cisco UCS Manager**
(1) Copy one row’s `dn` into the UCSM Equipment tree and open the same object the dn path names. Chassis and blade paths begin `sys/chassis-…`; rack units use `sys/rack-unit-…` if you extended the `rex` in a forked search; FI paths begin `sys/switch-…`.
(2) Compare magnitude and trend, not a frame-perfect match: UCSM’s GUI refresh and the add-on’s poll interval are not the same clock.
(3) If values disagree persistently, confirm you are not mixing cumulative energy counters with instantaneous power fields—Cisco’s MO reference for your firmware is decisive.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
(1) Row A on the dashboard: the table from the spl with colour rules on `status` (Critical red, Warn amber, NoBaseline grey, OK green if you add a `rangemap`).
(2) Row B: a timechart from a saved search clone filtered with `| search metric=chassis_input_power` for facilities kW review.
(3) Alerting: `| search status IN ("Critical","Warn")` on a 10- or 15-minute schedule after you are confident NoBaseline is only the inventory gap you expect. Throttle on `dn` and `metric`.
(4) For executive reporting, keep the 24h window on the main panel; for incident bridges, a 1h clone reduces noise on slowly moving hall temperatures.

**Step 5 — Tuning and troubleshooting**
• No `cisco:ucs:stats` at all — statistics modular input not running, 401/403, TLS trust, or index permissions. Check `splunkd.log` on the collection host and fix HTTPS before you tune the SPL.
• Missing a `classId` you need — the add-on’s enabled statistics class set is too small; add the missing MO class, wait two polls, re-run the classId count probe.
• All rows `NoBaseline` — typo in the CSV, lookup not in the app, or metric string mismatch. Compare your CSV metric column to the `metric` field in the table character-for-character.
• `chassis_num` is empty on some DNs — expected for some FI-only or IOM paths; the dn is still valid. Do not filter those rows out in the alert with `where isnotnull(chassis_num)` until you have confirmed your estate has no FI-anchored rules.
• Inlet thresholds copied onto CPU socket numbers — the lookup rows are different metrics on purpose; fix the CSV, not the Cisco hardware.
• PSU n+1 rotation or A/B feed work — a short watt dip can be a true electrical transient; if your facility team expects one-poll noise, add a `dedup` or a second-sample requirement in the alert.
• Fan RPM fine but a fault exists — pair with the fault and inventory use cases; pressure and redundancy live outside this stats-only view.
• Clock skew between service processor, UCSM, and Splunk `_time` — the dedupe in the saved search reduces flap; fix NTP on the management path for Sev1 trust.
• X-Series IFM and 65xx designs — additional module dn forms may need a one-line `rex` test in Search before a permanent change. Keep one governed pattern the NOC can recognize.
• Intersight-primary or HyperFlex per `exclusions` — if the cisco_ucs index goes quiet after migration, change the data product, not this alert’s CSV.
grandmaExplanation: We track how much power your shared racks draw, how hot the air and key parts are, and whether fans spin in a safe range, using a rule table you can edit for your room. We want early notice when cooling margin shrinks or power nears a budget, before hardware slows to protect itself or a hall team arrives too late in the day.
hardware: Cisco UCS B200 M5/M6/M7 blades; C220, C240, and C480 M5/M6/M7 rack units; X210c and X410c M6/M7 nodes in X9508; 6300/6400/6500 family Fabric Interconnects (6332, 6454, 6536); IOM 2204, 2208, 2304, 2408, and 9108 IFM for X-series.
